analysis.summary: Magento Open Source is a comprehensive PHP-based eCommerce platform that processes online transactions, manages product catalogs, and handles customer interactions through a modular architecture with over 200 integrated modules for storefront and admin functionality.
capabilities: Processes eCommerce transactions and order management (app/code/Magento/Sales/)
Manages product catalogs with inventory tracking (app/code/Magento/Catalog/, app/code/Magento/CatalogInventory/)
Handles customer registration and authentication (app/code/Magento/Customer/)
Provides admin dashboard and backend management (app/code/Magento/Backend/)
Processes payment transactions through multiple gateways (app/code/Magento/Payment/)
Manages content pages and blocks (app/code/Magento/Cms/)
Handles advanced search functionality (app/code/Magento/AdvancedSearch/, app/code/Magento/CatalogSearch/)
Supports multi-store and multi-currency operations (app/code/Magento/Store/, app/code/Magento/Directory/)
Manages shipping and tax calculations (app/code/Magento/Shipping/, app/code/Magento/Tax/)
Provides import/export capabilities for product data (app/code/Magento/ImportExport/)
Handles message queuing for asynchronous operations (app/code/Magento/AsynchronousOperations/, app/code/Magento/Amqp/)
Manages user roles and permissions (app/code/Magento/Authorization/, app/code/Magento/User/)
Processes analytics and reporting (app/code/Magento/Analytics/, app/code/Magento/Reports/)
technical_stack: XML for configuration and layout (etc/ directories)

technical_facts: Processes eCommerce transactions and order management (app/code/Magento/Sales/)
Manages product catalogs with inventory tracking (app/code/Magento/Catalog/, app/code/Magento/CatalogInventory/)
Handles customer registration and authentication (app/code/Magento/Customer/)
Provides admin dashboard and backend management (app/code/Magento/Backend/)
Processes payment transactions through multiple gateways (app/code/Magento/Payment/)
Manages content pages and blocks (app/code/Magento/Cms/)
Handles advanced search functionality (app/code/Magento/AdvancedSearch/, app/code/Magento/CatalogSearch/)
Supports multi-store and multi-currency operations (app/code/Magento/Store/, app/code/Magento/Directory/)
Manages shipping and tax calculations (app/code/Magento/Shipping/, app/code/Magento/Tax/)
Provides import/export capabilities for product data (app/code/Magento/ImportExport/)

architecture_patterns: Concurrency: synchronous with asynchronous message queue processing via RabbitMQ/AMQP for bulk operations
deployment_options: CI/CD: GitHub Actions (implied from repository structure)

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: Manages complex eCommerce workflows including order processing (app/code/Magento/Sales/), customer sessions (app/code/Magento/Customer/), inventory tracking (app/code/Magento/CatalogInventory/), and multi-store operations requiring state persistence across transactions
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: Uses XML schemas for configuration (etc/ directories), db_schema.xml files throughout modules for database structure, and REST/SOAP API endpoints with defined schemas (webapi.xml files)
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: Primary operations involve Create, Read, Update, Delete for products (app/code/Magento/Catalog/), orders (app/code/Magento/Sales/), customers (app/code/Magento/Customer/), and content management (app/code/Magento/Cms/)
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: eCommerce transactions require ACID compliance for order processing, payment handling (app/code/Magento/Payment/), inventory management (app/code/Magento/CatalogInventory/), and financial calculations (app/code/Magento/Tax/, app/code/Magento/Shipping/)
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: High modularity with 200+ modules and well-defined APIs makes components reusable, but deep eCommerce domain coupling (sales, shipping, tax calculations) limits direct application to other domains without significant modification

evidence_claims.evidence: composer.json files throughout the codebase specify PHP version requirements
db_schema.xml files throughout modules define database structure

evidence_claims.claim: Provides REST and SOAP API interfaces
evidence_claims.evidence: webapi.xml files define REST API endpoints at /rest/V1/* and SOAP endpoints at /soap/V1/*
app/code/Magento/AsynchronousOperations/ and app/code/Magento/Amqp/ modules for message queuing
app/code/Magento/AdvancedSearch/ and app/code/Magento/CatalogSearch/ modules with Elasticsearch integration (app/code/Magento/Elasticsearch/)

information_gaps: Specific database schema definitions for core entities
Detailed API rate limiting and throttling policies
Security implementation details beyond authentication methods
Specific version compatibility matrices for dependencies

analysis.summary: Sentry is an error tracking and performance monitoring platform built with Django/React that ingests, aggregates, and analyzes error events and performance data from client applications across multiple programming languages.
capabilities: Ingests and processes error events from client SDKs across multiple languages (src/sentry/receivers/, src/sentry/models/event.py)
Aggregates errors into issues with deduplication and grouping logic (src/sentry/grouping/, src/sentry/models/group.py)
Provides REST API for querying events, issues, projects, and organizations (src/sentry/api/endpoints/)
Renders React-based web interface for error analysis and project management (static/app/)
Processes and stores release deployments and source maps (src/sentry/models/release.py)
Manages user authentication and organization-based access control (src/sentry/auth/, src/sentry/models/organization.py)
Integrates with external services like Slack, GitHub, Jira for notifications (src/sentry/integrations/)
Supports SCIM provisioning for enterprise identity management (src/sentry/scim/)
Processes distributed tracing data for performance monitoring (src/sentry/spans/, src/sentry/traces/)
Generates alerts and notifications based on error patterns (src/sentry/rules/, src/sentry/notifications/)

technical_facts: Ingests and processes error events from client SDKs across multiple languages (src/sentry/receivers/, src/sentry/models/event.py)
Aggregates errors into issues with deduplication and grouping logic (src/sentry/grouping/, src/sentry/models/group.py)
Provides REST API for querying events, issues, projects, and organizations (src/sentry/api/endpoints/)

Generates alerts and notifications based on error patterns (src/sentry/rules/, src/sentry/notifications/)

architecture_patterns: Concurrency: async with Celery task queues for background processing, Django ASGI for async request handling

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: Ingests and processes error events from client SDKs across multiple languages (src/sentry/receivers/, src/sentry/models/event.py) - continuous event ingestion and real-time processing of error streams
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: REST API endpoints with defined paths and parameters (api-docs/openapi.json) but flexible event data structure to support multiple programming languages
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: Aggregates errors into issues with deduplication and grouping logic (src/sentry/grouping/, src/sentry/models/group.py) - pattern recognition and statistical grouping algorithms
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: Celery (pyproject.toml celery dependency, task processing) and Kafka (confluent-kafka in pyproject.toml, event streaming) - asynchronous processing architecture
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: High confidence due to generic event ingestion, aggregation, and alerting patterns that apply to many monitoring domains. Core streaming architecture is domain-agnostic.

complexity_factors: Multi-database architecture with PostgreSQL and ClickHouse

analysis.summary: Ghidra is a comprehensive software reverse engineering framework that disassembles, decompiles, and analyzes compiled binaries across multiple processor architectures and executable formats. It provides both interactive GUI tools and automated analysis capabilities through Java and Python APIs.
capabilities: Disassembles compiled binaries into assembly language (Ghidra/Features/Base/src/main/java/ghidra/app/plugin/core/disassembler)
Decompiles machine code into high-level C-like pseudocode (Ghidra/Features/Decompiler)
Analyzes executable formats including PE, ELF, Mach-O (Ghidra/Features/Base/src/main/java/ghidra/app/util/bin)
Supports multiple processor architectures through sleigh specification language (Ghidra/Processors)
Provides collaborative analysis through multi-user server architecture (Ghidra/Features/GhidraServer)
Executes user scripts in Java and Python for automated analysis (Ghidra/Features/Python, scripting directories)
Debugs live processes through various debugger integrations (Ghidra/Debug/Debugger-agent-*)
Parses and analyzes file system formats including DMG (GPL/DMG/src/dmg/java)
Demangles C++ and other mangled symbols (GPL/DemanglerGnu/src/demangler_gnu_v*)
Provides REST API for headless analysis operations (Ghidra/Features/Base/src/main/java/ghidra/framework/main/headless)
technical_stack: Python (scripting and debugger agents, 138 files with pyproject.toml dependencies)
Swing (GUI framework, evident from Java GUI classes)
JNI (Java Native Interface for C integration)
integrations: Various file format parsers (ELF, PE, Mach-O, DMG)

Demangles C++ and other mangled symbols (GPL/DemanglerGnu/src/demangler_gnu_v*)
Provides REST API for headless analysis operations (Ghidra/Features/Base/src/main/java/ghidra/framework/main/headless)

architecture_patterns: modular desktop application with optional server component
Concurrency: Multi-threaded with background analysis tasks, event-driven GUI updates, separate processes for debugger agents
dependencies: Infrastructure: Optional: Ghidra Server for collaborative analysis

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: Multi-threaded with background analysis tasks, event-driven GUI updates, separate processes for debugger agents - manages complex state across disassembly, decompilation, and debugging workflows
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: Analyzes executable formats including PE, ELF, Mach-O and uses sleigh specification language for processor architectures - requires strict binary format parsing
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: Disassembles compiled binaries into assembly language and decompiles machine code into high-level C-like pseudocode - requires control flow graph analysis and data flow analysis
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: Multi-user server architecture for collaborative analysis and multi-threaded background analysis tasks - supports eventual consistency across collaborative sessions
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: High confidence due to modular architecture with clear separation between parsing, analysis, and visualization components. The sleigh specification language and plugin system make it highly adaptable to different binary formats and analysis needs.

analysis.summary: Keycloak is an open-source identity and access management server that provides user authentication, authorization, and federation services. It processes authentication requests, manages user identities, and integrates with applications via SAML, OAuth2/OIDC protocols.
capabilities: Processes SAML authentication requests and generates responses (adapters/saml/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/SamlAuthenticator.java)
Manages user sessions and session stores (adapters/saml/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/SamlSessionStore.java)
Provides role mapping and authorization services (adapters/saml/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/RoleMappingsProvider.java)
Handles single sign-on (SSO) and single logout (SLO) flows (adapters/saml/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/profile/webbrowsersso/WebBrowserSsoAuthenticationHandler.java)
Parses and validates SAML configuration and metadata (adapters/saml/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/config/parsers/KeycloakSamlAdapterParser.java)
Implements cryptographic key rotation and management (adapters/saml/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/rotation/SamlDescriptorPublicKeyLocator.java)
Provides client adapters for Java EE/Jakarta EE applications (adapters/saml/wildfly-elytron/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/elytron/ElytronSamlAuthenticator.java)
integrations: External identity providers via SAML metadata

functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: Multi-threaded Java server with synchronous request processing, clustering support mentioned, session management across distributed instances
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: High confidence due to standard protocol implementations (SAML, OAuth2/OIDC) and modular adapter architecture. The authentication/authorization patterns are universally applicable across industries, with well-defined interfaces and extensible plugin system.
